entries.definition:
ظ/م/أ ẓ-m-ʾ thirst, to be thirsty, to cause to be thirsty; span of time, to be mean, to be unjust. Of this root, three forms occur once each in the Qurʾan: تَظْمَأُ taẓmaʾ, ظَمَأ ẓamaʾ and ظَمْآن ẓamʾān.
تَظْمَأُ taẓmaʾ [imperf. of v. intrans.] to become thirsty, to thirst (20:119) وَأَنَّكَ لَا تَظْمَأُ فِيهَا وَلَا تَضْحَى and [that] in it you will not become thirsty nor suffer the heat of the sun.

ظَمَأ ẓamaʾ [n.] thirst (9:120) لَا يُصِيبُهُمْ ظَمَأٌ وَلَا نَصَبٌ they do not suffer any thirst or weariness.

ظَمْآن ẓamʾān [quasi-act. part.] thirsty (24:39) يَحْسَبُهُ الظَّمْآنُ مَاءً the thirsty [one] thinks it to be water.
